values.content:
You are a grant eligibility and classification assistant. Given an organization with its location and narratives, and a list of grants, evaluate EACH
  grant individually.

  For EACH grant:

  STEP 1 - CLASSIFY THE GRANT TYPE:
  - Federal: Funded by federal agencies (HHS, SAMHSA, ACF, DOL, ED, HUD, USDA, etc.) or listed on Grants.gov. grant_state = null.
  - State: Funded by or restricted to a specific state. Identify the state from the grant details.
  - Philanthropic: From private foundations, trusts, corporate giving programs, or nonprofits. grant_state = null.

  STEP 2 - DETERMINE LOCATION ELIGIBILITY:
  - Federal grants: The organization IS eligible.
  - Philanthropic grants: The organization IS eligible.
  - State grants: The organization is ONLY eligible if it is located in or serves that state. Check geographic_focus and address. Be flexible with matching
  - abbreviations (NJ = New Jersey), typos, and partial matches count. If the org has no location data, give benefit of the doubt and mark eligible.

  STEP 3 - EVALUATE NICHE ALIGNMENT:
  Use the organization's narratives to understand what the org actually does — its mission, programs, populations served, and sector/industry focus. Then
  compare against EACH grant's title, agency, and description.

  - If the grant's focus area clearly matches the org's niche (e.g., both are about health, community services, maternal health, rural health, disability,
  youth/family services, education), mark niche_aligned = true.
  - If the grant is clearly for a completely different sector that the org does NOT work in (e.g., org does health/parent support but grant is for
  agriculture, military research, naval postgraduate, mining, forestry, commercial fishing, architecture biennales, mutant mouse genetics, cryo-electron
  tomography), mark niche_aligned = false.
  - Be generous but not absurd — there must be a plausible connection to the org's actual work.
  - If no narratives are available, default niche_aligned = true (benefit of the doubt).

  The grant must pass BOTH location eligibility AND niche alignment to be marked eligible.

  Return ONLY a valid JSON array (no markdown, no code fences) with one object per grant:
  [
    {
      "source_id": "the grant's source_id",
      "grant_type": "federal" or "state" or "philanthropic",
      "grant_state": "Full state name if state grant, otherwise null",
      "location_eligible": true or false,
      "niche_aligned": true or false,
      "eligible": true or false,
      "reason": "brief explanation"
    }
  ]

parameters.jsCode:
// Add additional major philanthropic foundations
const foundations = [
  {
    name: 'Bezos Earth Fund',
    ein: 'BEZOS',
    focus: 'Climate change, nature conservation, environmental justice',
    link: 'https://www.bezosearthfund.org/',
    floor: 100000,
    ceiling: 10000000
  },
  {
    name: 'Chan Zuckerberg Initiative',
    ein: 'CZI',
    focus: 'Science, education, community',
    link: 'https://chanzuckerberg.com/',
    floor: 50000,
    ceiling: 5000000
  },
  {
    name: 'The Ballmer Group',
    ein: 'BALLMER',
    focus: 'Economic mobility for children and families',
    link: 'https://www.ballmergroup.org/',
    floor: 25000,
    ceiling: 2000000
  },
  {
    name: 'Arnold Ventures',
    ein: 'ARNOLD',
    focus: 'Criminal justice, education, health, public finance',
    link: 'https://www.arnoldventures.org/',
    floor: 50000,
    ceiling: 3000000
  },
  {
    name: 'The Kresge Foundation',
    ein: '38-1359217',
    focus: 'Expanding opportunity in American cities',
    link: 'https://kresge.org/',
    floor: 25000,
    ceiling: 1000000
  },
  {
    name: 'The Annie E. Casey Foundation',
    ein: '52-0841143',
    focus: 'Child welfare, juvenile justice, economic opportunity',
    link: 'https://www.aecf.org/',
    floor: 25000,
    ceiling: 500000
  },
  {
    name: 'The California Endowment',
    ein: '95-4523232',
    focus: 'Health equity, healthy communities',
    link: 'https://www.calendow.org/',
    floor: 25000,
    ceiling: 1000000
  },
  {
    name: 'The JPB Foundation',
    ein: '27-6991959',
    focus: 'Poverty, environment, medical research',
    link: 'https://www.jpbfoundation.org/',
    floor: 50000,
    ceiling: 2000000
  },
  {
    name: 'The Heinz Endowments',
    ein: '25-1215856',
    focus: 'Creativity, environment, learning - PITTSBURGH/PA FOCUSED',
    link: 'https://www.heinz.org/',
    floor: 10000,
    ceiling: 500000
  },
  {
    name: 'William Penn Foundation',
    ein: '23-1503488',
    focus: 'Creative communities, watershed, great learning - PHILADELPHIA FOCUSED',
    link: 'https://williampennfoundation.org/',
    floor: 10000,
    ceiling: 1000000
  },
  {
    name: 'The Philadelphia Foundation',
    ein: '23-1365390',
    focus: 'Arts, education, human services - GREATER PHILADELPHIA',
    link: 'https://www.philafound.org/',
    floor: 5000,
    ceiling: 100000
  },
  {
    name: 'The Pittsburgh Foundation',
    ein: '25-0965466',
    focus: 'Community development, education, human services - PITTSBURGH REGION',
    link: 'https://pittsburghfoundation.org/',
    floor: 5000,
    ceiling: 100000
  }
];

const grants = foundations.map((f, i) => ({
  json: {
    id: f.ein + '-' + Date.now(),
    oppNum: f.ein.replace(/-/g, ''),
    title: f.name + ' - Philanthropic Foundation',
    description: 'Focus areas: ' + f.focus + '. Research their current RFPs and funding priorities.',
    link: f.link,
    openDate: new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0],
    closeDate: 'Ongoing - Check website',
    agency: f.name,
    agencyCode: f.ein,
    category: 'Major Foundation',
    source: 'Major Philanthropies',
    awardFloor: f.floor,
    awardCeiling: f.ceiling,
    status: 'active'
  }
}));

return grants;

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ success: false, message: $json.execution?.error?.message || "Proposal generation failed" }) }}
